identificationModule.briefTitle: Mindfulness to the Clinical Setting: The Mind Hand Connection Study
identificationModule.officialTitle: Mindfulness to the Clinical Setting: The Mind Hand Connection Study

responsibleParty.investigatorTitle: Chief, Division of Hospital Medicine & Assistant Professor of Medicine & Research Scientist

collaborators.name: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ)

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This is a single center, randomized study that assessed the effects of mindfulness training on physician teaching teams at a VA hospital.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: The primary objective of this study was to evaluate changes in hand hygiene compliance pre- and post- intervention in Internal Medicine physician and medical student inpatient teaching teams. Half of the teaching teams received the intervention of mindfulness education. Blinded observers collected data on hand hygiene compliance and mindfulness behaviors for both teams during patient care rounds for 1-week pre and post intervention. Additionally, the intervention group participated in focus group/interview discussions.

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Participating teams were randomized with a 1:1 ratio to receive the intervention.

maskingInfo.maskingDescription: Observers collecting data were blinded to participant treatment/control randomization allocation.

armGroups.label: Mindfulness Education
armGroups.description: Participants viewed an informational video titled, "All it Takes is 10 Mindful Minutes" by mindfulness expert, Andy Puddicombe. Following the video, a guided discussion was provided that focused on how the principles of mindfulness can be used to influence a physician's state of mental presence, allowing for moments of clarity, insight, and reflection, and potentially enhance provider and patient safety.

armGroups.label: No Intervention
armGroups.description: Participants did not receive mindfulness education.

primaryOutcomes.description: Change in hand hygiene compliance observed during patient care rounds
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline and study completion at 2 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Baseline and study completion at 2 weeks

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

Internal Medicine attendings, residents, interns and medical students on inpatient patient care teams, able to provide consent

Exclusion Criteria:

Surgical attendings, residents, interns or subspeciality teams, Internal medicine attendings rotating on non-resident teams

overallOfficials.name: Vineet I Chopra, MD
overallOfficials.affiliation: University of Michigan and Ann Arbor VA Healthcare System
